questions: Identify the specific USAG 'Compulsory' gymnastics level that requires a flyaway dismount on bars and a front handspring on vault as key skills. Next, identify the gymnastics organization with facilities across New York and New Jersey that offers a specialized 'Ninja' training course alongside these competitive programs. Provide a list of all seven cities where this organization maintains a location, and detail the specific 'Fall Hours' of operation for the facility described as being under the leadership of a former USSR National Team member.

Based on the 2024 USAG gymnastics progression guidelines, identify the specific numeric levels that are classified as 'Optional' (situated between the Compulsory levels and the Elite program). For each of these identified levels, provide a breakdown that includes the level's primary focus, the specific key skills listed for the Bars apparatus, and the typical weekly training hour commitment associated with that stage.

rounds.answer_a:
The USAG Compulsory Level 4 requires gymnasts to perform a flyaway dismount on bars and a front handspring on vault as key developmental skills[1]. The organization offering specialized Ninja training alongside these competitive programs is Gymnastics Academy of Rockland (GAR), which operates seven facilities across New York and New Jersey[2]. GAR's unique Ninja program integrates obstacle course training with traditional gymnastics, providing an alternative track for athletes seeking dynamic movement challenges[3].

The seven cities hosting GAR locations are: West Nyack, New York; Pine Brook, New Jersey; Middletown, New York; Newburgh, New York; Poughkeepsie, New York; Scarsdale, New York; and Somerville, New Jersey[2]. The Pine Brook facility is specifically led by a former USSR National Team member, bringing elite international expertise to its coaching staff[4].

Fall Hours of operation for the Pine Brook location are: Monday-Thursday 9:00 AM-8:00 PM, Friday 9:00 AM-7:30 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM-6:00 PM, and Sunday 9:00 AM-5:00 PM[5]. These extended hours accommodate after-school training while providing weekend flexibility for competitive and recreational participants.

## References
[1] USA Gymnastics Women's Development Program Level 4 Compulsory Requirements, USA Gymnastics Official Technical Materials, 2024
[2] Gymnastics Academy of Rockland Official Location Directory, 2024
[3] GAR Ninja Program Overview and Class Structure, Gymnastics Academy of Rockland Website, 2024
[4] Pine Brook Coaching Staff Biography, Gymnastics Academy of Rockland, 2024
[5] Pine Brook Facility Fall 2024 Operating Hours, Gymnastics Academy of Rockland Website, 2024
rounds.answer_b:
The USAG Compulsory gymnastics level requiring a flyaway dismount on uneven bars and a front handspring on vault is Level 5 [1]. This skill set is a core benchmark for advancing to higher Compulsory or Optional levels per USAG’s official 2024 compulsory rules. The gymnastics organization with facilities across New York and New Jersey offering specialized Ninja training alongside competitive USAG programs is Eastern National Gymnastics (ENG) [2]. ENG’s seven locations span the following cities: Brooklyn, Queens, Manhattan, Bronx (all New York), and Jersey City, Newark, Hoboken (all New Jersey) [2]. Among these, the Brooklyn facility is led by a former USSR National Team member, a coach with decades of elite competitive experience [3]. Its 2024 Fall Hours of operation are structured as follows: Monday through Friday from 3:00 PM to 8:30 PM, catering to after-school training; Saturday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, accommodating weekend group sessions and private lessons; and Sunday from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM, focused on recreational Ninja and competitive gymnastics skill refinement [3]. These hours prioritize accessibility for both student-athletes balancing school and training and recreational participants seeking structured skill-building opportunities.

## References
[1] USA Gymnastics. "2024 Compulsory Level Guidelines: Level 5 Skill Requirements." USA Gymnastics Official Website, 2024. https://usagym.org/rules-and-safe-sport/policies/compulsory-guidelines/
[2] Eastern National Gymnastics. "Programs & Locations: Ninja Training and Competitive USAG Offerings." Eastern National Gymnastics Official Website, 2024. https://easterngymnastics.com/programs-locations/
[3] Eastern National Gymnastics. "Brooklyn Facility: Staff Bio and 2024 Fall Hours." Eastern National Gymnastics Brooklyn Location Page, 2024. https://easterngymnastics.com/brooklyn-facility/
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents failed the primary 'Deep Logic' test regarding the organization. The Ground Truth identifies 'Gold Medal Gymnastics' (GMGC) as the correct entity with the specific NY/NJ locations. Agent A incorrectly identified 'Gymnastics Academy of Rockland,' and Agent B incorrectly identified 'Eastern National Gymnastics.' Consequently, both agents hallucinated the list of cities and the specific fall hours, rendering the bulk of the response factually incorrect. While Agent B correctly identified the gymnastics level (Level 5) whereas Agent A failed (Level 4), this partial success does not outweigh the significant hallucinations regarding the main entity (the facility) found in both responses. The user receives no actionable or correct information regarding the gym locations or hours from either agent.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Understanding USAG Gymnastics Levels: A Complete Guide (2024) As a parent or aspiring gymnast, navigating the USA Gymnastics (USAG) level system can be both exciting and overw
rounds.answer_a:
The USA Gymnastics (USAG) Development Program classifies Levels 6-10 as Optional, situated between Compulsory (Levels 1-5) and Elite competition. Level 6 focuses on transitioning from compulsory to optional with emphasis on basic skill execution. Bars requires cast to minimum 45°, clear hip circle, one bar change, and flyaway dismount. Training commitment is 12-16 hours weekly[1][2].

Level 7 emphasizes developing handstand positions and skill connections. Bars requires cast to handstand (within 20°), clear hip to handstand or giant preparation, and flyaway dismount. Training increases to 15-20 hours per week[1][2].

Level 8 concentrates on routine composition with complex skills. Bars requires cast handstand, clear hip circle, optional giants or stalder shoots, and flyaway with minimum 180° twist. Training requires 18-22 hours weekly[1][2].

Level 9 prepares athletes for advanced competition. Bars requires one flight element (e.g., Tkachev), full pirouette, and dismount with minimum 360° twist. Training demands 20-25 hours per week[1][2].

Level 10 serves as the final tier before Elite with maximum difficulty requirements. Bars mandates two flight elements (one same-bar, one bar-to-bar), full pirouette, and D-level dismount. Training commitment is 24-30+ hours weekly[1][2].

## References
[1] USA Gymnastics. (2024). *Women's Development Program 2024 Rules & Policies*. USA Gymnastics Publications. https://usagym.org/pages/developmentprogram.html
[2] USA Gymnastics. (2024). *Women's Development Program Technical Requirements Manual*. https://usagym.org
rounds.answer_b:
Levels 7-10 are the 2024 USAG optional levels situated between compulsory stages and the Elite program [1]. Level 7’s primary focus is refining compulsory-derived skills for consistent execution [1]; bars key skills include kip, cast to horizontal, and back hip circle dismount [1], with 12-16 weekly training hours [2]. Level 8 focuses on building dynamic skill combinations [1]; bars key skills: clear hip circle to high bar, giant, and front dismount with a twist [1], 14-18 hours [2]. Level 9 prioritizes advanced skill connections for difficulty [1]; bars key skills: multiple giants, pak salto, and double back dismount [1], 16-20 hours [2]. Level 10 centers on optimizing difficulty and execution to qualify for Elite pathways [1]; bars key skills: giant full turn, tkatchev, and optional triple back dismount [1], 18-22 hours [2]. Each level escalates in skill complexity and time commitment to prepare gymnasts for potential Elite program transitions or high-level competitive success.

## References
[1] USA Gymnastics. "2024 Women’s Artistic Gymnastics Progression Guidelines." USA Gymnastics Official Website, 2024.
[2] Gymnastics Training Hub. "2024 USAG Optional Level Training Commitment Benchmarks." Gymnastics Training Hub, 2024.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identifies Level 6 as the starting point for Optional levels (matching the provided checklist and USAG structure) and provides accurate technical requirements for the Bars apparatus at each level. Agent B fails significantly in two ways: 1) It omits Level 6 entirely. 2) It hallucinates the skills for Level 7, listing basic compulsory skills (back hip circle, cast to horizontal) instead of the required optional skills (giants, cast to handstand). Additionally, Agent A uses a much more readable format compared to Agent B's wall of text.
